text: The strict mapping creates three problems.
pronounce_text: The strict mapping creates three problems.

text: First, every pixel has a precise spatial meaning. If the arm texture drifts by even one pixel, the 3D character can look broken.
pronounce_text: First, every pixel has a precise spatial meaning. If the arm texture drifts by even one pixel, the three D character can look broken.

text: Second, the model has to understand the relationship between the 2D UV map and the 3D body. A scarf, a coat, or a strand of hair might cross multiple components.
pronounce_text: Second, the model has to understand the relationship between the two D UV map and the three D body. A scarf, a coat, or a strand of hair might cross multiple components.

text: If the model does not understand the geometry, the detail can appear on the wrong face or disappear entirely.
pronounce_text: If the model does not understand the geometry, the detail can appear on the wrong face or disappear entirely.

text: Third, Minecraft skins depend on transparency. The alpha channel decides which outer-layer pixels should be visible. Most diffusion image models generate RGB images by default, not clean RGBA texture files.
pronounce_text: Third, Minecraft skins depend on transparency. The alpha channel decides which outer-layer pixels should be visible. Most diffusion image models generate RGB images by default, not clean RGBA texture files.

text: So the model is not just learning "Minecraft style." It is learning a constrained 2D-to-3D encoding system.
pronounce_text: So the model is not just learning "Minecraft style. " It is learning a constrained two D to three D encoding system.
